text: HORMUZ CLASH DETAILS: US Navy destroyers Truxtun, Rafael Peralta, Mason came under IRGC attack with missiles, drones, small boats. CENTCOM struck back at Qeshm Island (Bahman Pier), Bandar Abbas, Bandar Khamir, Sirik. 6 IRGC boats destroyed. No US casualties confirmed. Iran claims 'significant damage' to US ships — disputed by Pentagon.
verificationNote: NPR and CENTCOM statement confirmed engagement details. Iran claims from PressTV.

text: ASSESSMENT: Trump calling Hormuz strikes 'just a love tap' while threatening 'one big glow' if no deal signals dual-track approach — maintain ceasefire fiction while escalating military pressure. Iran PGSA (Strait Authority) creates fait accompli: even if deal opens Hormuz, Iran claims permanent toll authority. Significant precedent.
verificationNote: CNN and Fox News confirmed Trump quotes. Maritime Executive confirmed PGSA details.

text: LEAK: WaPo satellite analysis shows 228 structures damaged at US bases — far more than Pentagon acknowledged. Includes hangars, barracks, fuel depots, aircraft, radar, comms equipment. 7 US KIA, 400+ wounded by late April. Meanwhile CIA assessment says Iran retains 75% mobile launchers, 70% missile stocks. Pressure campaign not working as advertised.
verificationNote: Washington Post investigations team published both reports with satellite imagery.

text: DEAL TRACKER: 14-point MOU framework close to final. Key sticking points: enrichment moratorium length (US wants 20yr, Iran offers 5yr, likely landing 12-15yr). Sanctions relief timeline. Hormuz reopening sequence. Witkoff/Kushner negotiating via Pakistan mediation. Iran reviewing US proposal — response expected within 48 hours. Ceasefire expires May 17.
verificationNote: Axios and CNN confirmed MOU framework and negotiation details.

text: ISRAEL SECURITY CHIEFS: Unanimous opposition to emerging US-Iran deal, calling it 'disaster for Israel.' Fear Iran would deceive US, rush to nukes, seize billions in released funds. Netanyahu published 4 red lines: remove ALL enriched material, dismantle enrichment, restrict ballistic missiles, Israel keeps freedom of action.
verificationNote: Haaretz reported security chiefs' opposition. Israel Hayom confirmed 4 red lines.

text: MARKETS RALLY: S&P 500 record 7,365.12 (+1.46%). Dow +600pts. Markets pricing in peace deal. But oil still elevated — Brent $101.26, WTI $95.64 — Hormuz physically closed since May 4. Markets treating war as past tense while Strait remains shut. Disconnect could correct violently if deal falls through.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed S&P record and oil prices.

text: IRAN DOMESTIC: Internet shutdown enters 70th day per NetBlocks. Pezeshkian met Mojtaba Khamenei for 2.5 hours — seeks to quash 'divided leadership' narrative. But Pezeshkian earlier called IRGC UAE strikes 'completely irresponsible' and 'madness.' Civil-military divide in Iran is real despite show of unity.
verificationNote: CNN cited NetBlocks. Al Jazeera confirmed Pezeshkian-Khamenei meeting. Iran International reported IRGC criticism.

text: LEBANON FRONT COLLAPSE: IDF killed Hezbollah Radwan commander Ballout in Beirut (first strike since April 16 ceasefire). Hezbollah retaliated with record 105 attack waves — 1 killed, 14 wounded in Nahariya. Rockets at Karmiel, W. Galilee, Haifa Bay. IDF struck ~20 targets overnight. Ceasefire effectively dead. Peace talks round 3 still planned May 14-15.
verificationNote: Times of Israel and Media Line confirmed Nahariya casualties. JPost confirmed Ballout killing.

text: CHINA PLAY: Araghchi in Beijing — Wang Yi proposes 'four shared homelands' peace concept. Araghchi endorses it. Key timing: 1 week before Trump-Xi summit May 14-15. China positioning as alternative mediator. First Chinese tanker (JV Innovation) attacked May 4 — Beijing publicly angered. China-Iran trade already down 50% YoY.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera confirmed Beijing meeting. Caixin Global confirmed Chinese tanker attack.

text: UAE UNDER FIRE AGAIN: UAE intercepted fresh Iranian missiles/drones May 8. Dubai schools on distance learning Day 4. Bahrain requested UNSC closed consultation May 6 after first Iranian strikes on UAE since April 8 ceasefire. 3 Indian nationals wounded at Fujairah Oil Zone May 4. Arrow-3 crisis: Knesset emergency meeting — Iran making missiles faster than Israel producing interceptors.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed UAE intercepts. Security Council Report confirmed Bahrain UNSC request. Haaretz confirmed Arrow-3 gap.

text: SANCTIONS SQUEEZE: May 1 sanctions hit 6 individuals, 21 entities including Chinese petroleum terminal (Qingdao Haiye). 12th round since Feb 2025. Iranian rial collapsed to 1.789M/USD (free market) — down 55% in 12 months. China-Iran trade down 50% YoY Q1 2026. But CIA says Iran can outlast blockade 3-4 months using overland smuggling.
verificationNote: US Treasury confirmed sanctions. WaPo confirmed CIA assessment. Bonbast confirmed rial rate.

text: OIL UPDATE: Brent $100.54 (+25.9% pre-war), WTI $95.46 (+26.4%). Off yesterday's highs. S&P 500 record 7,365 on deal hopes. Gas $4.55 (AAA). OPEC+ 188K bpd increase for June (without UAE, quit May 1). IEA: 412M barrels from strategic reserves. Hormuz Day 67 blockade — zero transits since May 4.
verificationNote: Trading Economics confirmed oil prices. AAA confirmed gas. CNBC confirmed S&P record.

text: DAY 70 AM ASSESSMENT: 3 IDF soldiers wounded by Hezbollah FPV explosive drones in S. Lebanon (1 seriously). IDF retaliated with strikes across Nabatieh, Ain Baal, Dibbin. Lebanon-Israel talks still on for May 14-15 in Washington despite ceasefire collapse. Key question: can compartmentalized diplomacy survive escalating military reality?
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed IDF casualties. Arab News confirmed Washington talks.

text: MARITIME: Lloyd's confirms Hormuz 'closed' — zero transits since May 4. 22,500 mariners trapped on 1,550+ vessels. 147 containerships (470K TEU) stuck. French CMA CGM San Antonio attacked May 5 with crew injuries. Ship operators say they'll 'remain cautious until stable, predictable' situation. South Korea routing 4th tanker via Red Sea alternative.
verificationNote: NBC News and Jerusalem Post confirmed shipping data. UPI confirmed South Korean tanker.

text: WAR COSTS REALITY CHECK: Pentagon says $25B — Harvard Kennedy School says 'certain we will reach $1 trillion.' WaPo: 228 structures at US bases damaged (not officially acknowledged). Trump requesting $1.5T defense budget (42% increase, largest since WWII). Iran losing $500M/day from blockade. Israel $11.5B. Iran self-assessed $300B-$1T damage.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, CNN, CNBC confirmed cost figures. WaPo confirmed base damage investigation.

text: POPE-RUBIO SUMMIT: Rubio met Pope Leo XIV at Vatican for 45 min — 'frank conversation' on Iran war disagreements. Pope gave olive-wood pen as peace symbol. Earlier Pope surprised 13 Lebanese priests via video: 'Peace is near.' Trump had accused Pope of 'endangering' Catholics. Vatican-US tensions over war continuing to escalate.
verificationNote: CNN, NPR, Washington Times confirmed Vatican meeting and video call details.

text: FOOD IMPACT: USDA forecasts food-at-home +2.5% for 2026. Farm wheat up 8.4%. Palm/soybean oil each +8%. Tehran wheat flour up 120% in one month, nearly 200% YoY. IRC operational costs spiked 50% due to war disruptions. Average US grocery bill up $14/week ($182 vs $168 pre-war). If Hormuz persists through summer, food-at-home could add 5-7 more percentage points.
verificationNote: USDA ERS confirmed food price forecasts. FAO confirmed commodity data. IRC confirmed cost spike.
